description:
You are a backup-verification engineer.  
    A recent backup has been staged in the directory  

        /home/user/daily_backup  

    The directory tree already exists and looks like this:

        /home/user/daily_backup/docs/report.txt
        /home/user/daily_backup/images/logo.png
        /home/user/daily_backup/data/data.csv

    For each file, a reference SHA-256 digest is stored (one digest per line, in the format produced by the standard “sha256sum” utility) inside  

        /home/user/reference/sha256sums.txt  

    Your job is to check the integrity of every file in /home/user/daily_backup against the digests in /home/user/reference/sha256sums.txt and write a very small, human-readable report.

    1. Create the directory  

           /home/user/integrity_logs  

       if it does not already exist.  
    2. Generate a log file named  

           /home/user/integrity_logs/backup_check.log  

       following **exactly** the format shown below (no extra spaces, tabs, headers, or blank lines):

           docs/report.txt OK
           images/logo.png OK
           data/data.csv OK

       • Use **relative paths** that are *relative to* /home/user/daily_backup.  
       • The word “OK” must appear when the calculated SHA-256 digest for the file matches the reference digest.  
       • If any file were to fail the check, the word must be “MISMATCH” instead, but in this particular run every file is expected to pass.

    Only a single terminal command (pipeline/compound commands allowed) should ultimately be needed to produce the correct log, but you may reason step-by-step before forming it.  When you are done, the file /home/user/integrity_logs/backup_check.log must exist and match the exact three-line format above; automated tests will check its presence and contents verbatim.
truth:
Initial files and their exact textual contents (a trailing newline is present in each text file):

      1. /home/user/daily_backup/docs/report.txt
         ----------------------------
         Quarterly Financial Report
         Q1 Revenue: $1,200,000
         Q1 Profit: $250,000
         ----------------------------

      2. /home/user/daily_backup/images/logo.png  
         (7-byte ASCII text) “PNGDATA” followed by a newline.

      3. /home/user/daily_backup/data/data.csv
         ----------------------------
         id,value
         1,alpha
         2,beta
         3,gamma
         ----------------------------

    The file /home/user/reference/sha256sums.txt contains three lines in standard “sha256sum” format whose digests match the files above.  Each digest is followed by two spaces and the *relative* path starting from daily_backup (e.g. “docs/report.txt”).

    Expected final artefact generated by the agent:

      /home/user/integrity_logs/backup_check.log
      ------------------------------------------------
      docs/report.txt OK
      images/logo.png OK
      data/data.csv OK
      ------------------------------------------------
      (A single trailing newline is acceptable but not required.)

    The automated test will verify:
      • that the directory /home/user/integrity_logs exists,
      • that the file backup_check.log exists inside it,
      • and that its content is byte-for-byte identical to the three-line block above (ignoring an optional final newline).
